caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The sources survey developers or development organizations, not occupations within US NAICS 541511.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is four-digit 5415 and understates the code-delivery concentration of a pure custom-programming shop.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: This input measures technical task replaceability only; it does not take credit for adoption speed, client demand or pricing.
inputs.pi_dist: ISG covers broader application-development outsourcing and Globant is a scaled provider, not a US lower-middle-market 541511 sample.
inputs.pi_dist: No fresh source measures the T&M, staff-augmentation or fixed-price mix for the target EBITDA band.
inputs.pi_moat: The source includes managed services and global systems integrators, which often have stickier contracts than a project-oriented 541511 firm.
inputs.pi_moat: This is a capture input only and does not use human accountability to claim a terminal-demand floor.
inputs.t50_years: Tool use and self-reported productivity are not identical to effective adoption across all billable tasks.
inputs.t50_years: The evidence is not a small-firm, NAICS-specific longitudinal adoption measure.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: GitHub's survey was 500 US developers at companies with 1,000+ employees and predates the current tool generation.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Stack Overflow and DORA do not isolate US lower-middle-market custom-programming firms.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is qualitative context only and is not used to set adoption timing beyond the separate adoption inputs.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not a measured owner-age rate and should not be read as evidence of a succession wave.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of a located source is not evidence that no individual firm faces succession risk.
inputs.active_consolidators: The evidence covers broad IT services, including MSPs, cloud and systems integration rather than a deal tally for 541511.
inputs.active_consolidators: This factor is used only for buyability and does not support adoption, capture or terminal demand.
inputs.buy_mult: This is a UK IT-services benchmark, not disclosed US lower-middle-market 541511 transactions.
inputs.buy_mult: Contractor pass-through and owner compensation normalization can materially change an apparent EBITDA multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct evidence supports multiple expansion for a generic 541511 firm; the selected exit is conditional on contract and revenue-quality improvement.
inputs.exit_mult: The source mix includes managed services and cloud assets with more recurring revenue than custom programming.
inputs.pricing_structure: No source quantifies the current pricing-model mix for US $1-10M EBITDA 541511 operators.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $292,242 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541511); class <5: 45,975 firms, avg revenue/firm $413,913 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $64,777 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 6,238 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,817,988 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $284,515 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 4,153 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,749,458 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $586,790 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 4,523 firms, avg revenue/firm $11,255,622 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,761,505 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 1,244 firms, avg revenue/firm $52,288,390 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,183,133 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 640 firms, avg revenue/firm $224,003,744 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $35,056,586 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 5,767

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI coding use and productivity evidence supports substantial task compression in code, tests and documentation, while trust, review and client-accountability limits keep each role fraction below full automation.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541511 ($192,866,921,104) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541511 ($305,260,086,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass and all seven score-driving sources resolve. The sources support the thesis direction and cited market facts, while the packet explicitly discloses that adoption, pricing, buyer, and multiple evidence is drawn from broader developer, ADM, global-provider, UK IT-services, or four-digit occupational frames. Those limitations warrant publication caveats but do not demonstrate a hidden score-changing defect, reversed factor, or capture/terminal double count.

sources.title: 2025 Stack Overflow Developer Survey — AI
sources.what_it_supports: Current developer AI-tool/agent use, reported productivity effects, and accuracy/security constraints.

sources.title: GitHub survey: AI's impact on developer experience
sources.what_it_supports: Enterprise-developer survey evidence on AI coding-tool use, perceived benefits, code review and collaboration remaining in the workflow.

sources.title: DORA: Impact of Generative AI in Software Development (v2025.2)
sources.what_it_supports: Reported productivity, the role of trust, and continuing needs for review, testing and responsible use in development.

sources.title: ISG Provider Lens: AI-driven ADM Services 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Application-development outsourcing pricing transition from time-and-materials/fixed price toward outcome, experience and subscription structures, plus broader accountability in contracts.

sources.title: Globant AI Pods announcement
sources.what_it_supports: A scaled IT-services provider's stated move to subscription, token-metered engineering pricing aligned to outcomes rather than effort.

sources.title: Accenture fiscal 2025 financial performance
sources.what_it_supports: Strategic buyer activity: Accenture states it invested about $1.5 billion across 23 strategic acquisitions in fiscal 2025 and reports AI services bookings/revenue.

sources.title: Alinea IT Services Spring 2025 sector reflections
sources.what_it_supports: IT-services M&A volume, average EBITDA multiple and recurring-revenue/MSP premium caveat for service-business multiple selection.

title: Custom Computer Programming Services
